Explanation: Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the Person table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Explicitly list only the required columns, e.g., .select(Person.PERSON.ID, Person.PERSON.NAME).

Code fragment: public final TableField<PersonRecord, Long> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key is named just 'id', which is a generic name that doesn't convey any meaning about the entity it identifies. Consider renaming it to 'person_id' to make it more descriptive and to avoid ambiguity when joining tables.
